title: Deterministic fills: model picks palette, code paints
oneline: End-to-end success: perfect registration, coherent themes, mermaid rivals shipped fill
summary: Flood-fill segmentation of the pen outlines sealed every stroke-bounded region with ZERO gap-closing on all three pages (28/34/80 regions); the only enclosure failure was compositional (circle-tall's open grass-mound arc), fixed by a hierarchical background-only coarse split. One gemini-2.5-flash vision call per page labeled a numbered region map against a 31-name curated palette with matched light/night hexes, and programmatic painting + the punch's ring bleed produced shipped-form fills scoring 100% keep/localKeep. The hard page (mermaid) is visually competitive with the shipped model-painted fill; the simplest page (circle) looks flattest, where the model's soft gradients did the most work.
tried: Flood-fill segmentation of pen outlines at native res with punch-threshold ink bar (luma<150)
Morphological close radius sweep (0/4/8/12) for gap sealing
Hierarchical refine: coarse close-12 partition splits the fine background region only
Numbered random-tint region map + per-region stats to gemini-2.5-flash, strict-JSON region->palette plan covering light+night in one call
Programmatic paint + punch-style ring bleed producing shipped fills-only form directly
outline-match gates and multiply/night-composite comparisons vs shipped fills
worked: Strokes seal at close radius 0 on all three pages — normalized outlines are watertight; segmentation never exploded (mermaid: 66 labeled regions, all correctly identified by the model)
100% keep / 100% localKeep on all six fills — registration perfect by construction
One theme-neutral palette name per region gives perfect light/night coherence for free
Vision labeling at 66 regions: hair/pupils/catchlights/shell bra/scales/rock all named right
Anti-bias prompt line fixed tint leakage (purple-tinted face had been colored lavender; became skin-peach)
Background-only coarse split sealed the open grass mound without harming interior detail
failed: Global morphological close (r=12) swallowed eye whites, catchlights, bubble highlights — unusable alone
Splitting ALL coarse-divided regions shredded the mermaid's hair into inconsistently-colored fragments
First mermaid plan colored the face lavender — random region tints bias the model without an explicit warning
limitations: Flat fills lose gradients/soft shading; simplest pages (circle) suffer most — hybrid one-pass shading would reintroduce the drift risk and need re-gating
Open compositions (not stroke gaps) are the real segmentation risk; background-only split covered the one case here
Color-plan variance across calls (same page: sky-blue vs tan background) — plans should be committed JSON artifacts
Hand-picked night palette runs darker than shipped night fills; needs one global calibration pass
Regions <80px are bled over; a meaningful tiny feature under the bar would be erased
